title: NOC 85121 – Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 85121 covers the essential workforce that builds and maintains Canada's urban green spaces, residential gardens, and public parks. As a TEER 5 occupation, these labourers perform the fundamental manual tasks required for landscape construction and grounds upkeep. In 2026, as Canadian cities invest heavily in "Green Infrastructure" to combat urban heat and manage stormwater, these labourers are in constant demand. For immigration, this role is a popular entry point through the New 2026 TEER 5 PR Pathway and various Provincial "In-Demand" streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level5Major Group85 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersSub-Major Group851 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersMinor Group8512 – Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourersNOC Code85121

duties: Landscape Construction: Assist in the construction of gardens, walls, fences, and patios; spread topsoil, lay sod, and plant trees, shrubs, and flowers.
Grounds Upkeep: Cut grass, edge lawns, and weed gardens using rakes, mowers, and other turf care equipment.
Maintenance: Prune shrubs and trees; assist with the installation and repair of irrigation and drainage systems.
Watering: Maintain the health of plants by watering and applying fertilizers or organic mulch.
Winter Operations: Remove snow from sidewalks, driveways, and parking lots using shovels, snowblowers, and salt spreaders.
Equipment Care: Clean and maintain work areas and specialized landscaping tools/machinery.
employment_requirements: No formal education is strictly required, though some secondary school is preferred.
On-the-Job Training: Employers provide short-term practical training on tool usage and safety.
Certifications:A valid driver's license is often required to move between job sites.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) for handling fertilizers.
A valid driver's license is often required to move between job sites.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) for handling fertilizers.
Physical stamina and the ability to work outdoors in various weather conditions (heat, rain, and cold) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Workers specialized in "Hardscaping" (stone and wood construction) often earn closer to the "High" range due to the technical nature of the work.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Industry data suggests that the landscaping sector will see a 9% growth in job openings over the next decade. As Canadian municipalities increase their "Urban Canopy" targets to reach climate goals, the demand for labourers to plant and maintain millions of new city trees remains a high priority for local governments and private contractors.
labour_market_demand: The demand for landscaping labourers is Very Good, particularly in provinces with high residential development and tourism. In 2026, Ontario (GTA), British Columbia, and Quebec are reporting significant labor shortages. While the work is traditionally seasonal (spring to autumn), many Canadian firms now offer year-round employment by transitioning these labourers into Snow and Ice Management during the winter months.
High Accessibility: No formal education or prior experience is mandatory, making it an ideal first job for new immigrants.
Health & Wellness: Work outdoors in some of Canada’s most beautiful environments, from high-end golf courses to municipal botanical gardens.
Skill Diversification: Gain hands-on experience in both "Softscaping" (planting/gardening) and "Hardscaping" (interlocking stone/deck construction).
Bridge to Skilled Trades: This role provides the foundation to become a Landscape Supervisor (NOC 82031) or a certified Horticulturist.
pathways: New 2026 TEER 4 & 5 Pathway: A newly launched federal stream targeting essential workers with at least one year of Canadian work experience.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Accessible for those working in the province's tourism or designated sectors for at least 9 months.
OINP In-Demand Skills Stream: Targeted at workers in specific landscaping roles with a job offer in Ontario.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for workers with job offers from designated landscaping firms in the Atlantic region.
